draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers warm, ambient lighting over harsh bright workspaces
facade.label: Soft glow
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward cozy, low-contrast interfaces with warm color palettes and gentle lighting effects
facade.rejectImplies: Explore crisp, high-contrast, cooler-toned designs with bright, efficient illumination

facade.hypothesis: Dense, tabular data presentation appeals more than narrative summary for AI workspace users
facade.label: Numbers in boxes
facade.acceptImplies: Users want to scan structured, quantified information quickly—tables, grids, scorecards over prose
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer storytelling, context, and narrative flow—prose, highlights, and supporting explanation over raw numbers

evidence.hypothesis: The user prefers warm, ambient lighting over harsh bright workspaces
evidence.implication: Lean toward cozy, low-contrast interfaces with warm color palettes and gentle lighting effects

facade.hypothesis: Users expect a persistent left sidebar for navigation paired with content cards in a main area
facade.label: Sidebar + cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore multi-column layouts, card-based content organization, persistent navigation patterns
facade.rejectImplies: Pivot toward full-width tabs, list-view primary content, collapsible/hidden navigation

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer snappy, responsive interactions over smooth, animated transitions
facade.label: Instant vs fluid
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should prioritize instant feedback and crisp state changes—no unnecessary easing or motion delays
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace should embrace fluid animations, gentle transitions, and motion that feels deliberate and considered rather than reactive

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer low-light, high-contrast visual environments that reduce cognitive load during intense focus work
facade.label: Dark mode interface
facade.acceptImplies: Design should feature dark backgrounds, bright text, minimal glare—possibly with customizable contrast levels for accessibility
facade.rejectImplies: Design should explore lighter, more neutral palettes or context-dependent lighting that adapts to different workspace zones

facade.hypothesis: Users want an AI workspace that feels approachable and human, not corporate or robotic, while still delivering professional-grade functionality.
facade.label: Casual tone, serious product
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into conversational copy, warm microcopy, approachable error messages, and a tone that doesn't take itself too seriously—even as the product does serious work.
facade.rejectImplies: Stick with neutral, polished, corporate voice. Minimize personality. Let the interface speak for itself without friendly language or humor.

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers spacious, layered interfaces with breathing room over dense, grid-locked layouts
facade.axisTargeted: organic vs digital / expansive vs intimate
facade.label: Floating panels
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward modular card-based designs with negative space, floating elements, and visual hierarchy through depth rather than proximity
facade.rejectImplies: User favors compact, efficient layouts where information density and structured grids take priority over visual breathing room

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses
